articles.title: China’s DeepSeek previews new AI model a year after jolting US rivals
articles.summary: Chinese AI company DeepSeek released a preview of its hotly anticipated next-generation AI model V4 on Friday, saying that the open-source model can compete with leading closed-source systems from US rivals including Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI. articles.title: OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 model is more efficient and better at coding
articles.summary: OpenAI just announced its new GPT-5.5 model, which the company calls its "smartest and most intuitive to use model yet, and the next step toward a new way of getting work done on a computer." OpenAI just released GPT-5.4 last month, but says that the new GPT-5.5 "excels" at...

articles.title: Show HN: A Karpathy-style LLM wiki your agents maintain (Markdown and Git)
articles.summary: I shipped a wiki layer for AI agents that uses markdown + git as the source of truth, with a bleve (BM25) + SQLite index on top. No vector or graph db yet. It runs locally in ~/.wuphf/wiki/ and you can git clone it out if you want to take your knowledge with you. The shape is...

articles.title: Show HN: GoModel – an open-source AI gateway in Go
articles.summary: Describe a task, and AI will give you a breakdown of whether it can do your task well, poorly, or somewhere in between. I built it mostly because I kept getting asked "what is AI even good for" and fumbling the answer. The most fun use is testing it on things you already know it...

articles.title: Show HN: Browser Harness – Gives LLM freedom to complete any browser task
articles.summary: Hey HN, We got tired of browser frameworks restricting the LLM, so we removed the framework and gave the LLM maximum freedom to do whatever it's trained on. We gave the harness the ability to self correct and add new tools if the LLM wants (is pre-trained on) that. Our Browser...

articles.title: Show HN: Built a daily game where you sort historical events chronologically
articles.summary: Hey folks, I built Hisorty, a small daily game where you sort historical events in chronological order (strongly inspired by Wordle mechanics). Each day you get 6 events and 3 attempts to place them correctly. I found it surprisingly tricky to design those puzzles so the...

articles.title: Show HN: Claude Code Manager
articles.summary: I built this for myself but I figured why not share. The aim of CCM is to be able to fully manage all Claude Code configuration files, both globally and those in your project. Some neat features: - Manages your CLAUDE.md, rules, hooks, agents, memories and so on. - Elevate...

articles.title: Show HN: FalsoAI – Detect influence/manipulation patterns in content
articles.summary: I built this tool because I believe AI can be used to protect people from social engineering and influence/manipulation patterns. In a way, it is similar to what an antivirus does for a computer, but applied to human cognition. This simple project is mainly an MVP proof of...

articles.summary: I went crazy with a tool that helps me tracing raster images. Thought other might like it. It doesn't auto vectorize image, but rather allow for guided process. Final SVG still should be edited. Few fun features like genetic algorithm fit optimization, semi-manual tracing and...

articles.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
articles.summary: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

articles.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
articles.summary: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

articles.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
articles.summary: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

articles.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
articles.summary: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

articles.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
articles.summary: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

articles.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
articles.summary: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

articles.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
articles.summary: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

articles.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
articles.summary: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

articles.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
articles.summary: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

articles.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
articles.summary: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

articles.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
articles.summary: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.
